title: Geometric part: i_K^* must commute with pi_j for layer geometricity, and Postnikov assembly (a limit) needs convergence to land in tau

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Gap: Postnikov-tower assembly of the geometric part conflates spectrum-level and layer-level geometricity, and treats a limit as a colimit

This refines the concern in q5-8, isolating two distinct unproved steps in `lem:geompart` and its use in the converse.

**(A) From $i_K^*X\simeq 0$ to "each homotopy Mackey functor is geometric".**
The solution proves $i_K^*(\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes E)\simeq 0$ for proper $K$ (spectrum level) and then declares each $\underline\pi_j(\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes E)$ "geometric" in the sense of Definition `def:geom`, which demands $i_K^*\,H\underline\pi_j\simeq 0$. The passage from the spectrum vanishing to the *layer's EM-spectrum* vanishing requires the (true but unstated) fact that $i_K^*$ commutes with the genuine homotopy Mackey functors:
$$ i_K^*\,\underline\pi_j(X)\ =\ \underline\pi_j(i_K^*X), $$
so that $i_K^*X\simeq0\Rightarrow i_K^*\underline\pi_j(X)=0\Rightarrow i_K^*H\underline\pi_j(X)\simeq0$. This commutation (exactness/colimit-preservation of $i_K^*$ on $t$-structure hearts) is never invoked, yet it is exactly what makes Definition `def:geom`'s hypothesis hold for the layers.

**(B) A Postnikov tower is a limit; closure of $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$ under it is not automatic.**
`lem:geompart` concludes $\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes E\in\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge c[G:c]}$ by asserting it "is built by a finite/transfinite sequence of cofiber sequences out of these layers", using closure of $\tau$ under extensions and homotopy colimits. But the Postnikov tower $\{\tau_{\le j}X\}$ is a tower under **limits**, and $X\to\varprojlim$ being an equivalence (convergence) plus identifying $X$ with a **colimit** of objects built from the layers is precisely the bounded-below convergence statement that is not established here (it is the same gap flagged for the main tower in q5-14). A localizing subcategory is closed under colimits and extensions, *not* under arbitrary limits; so one must explicitly present $\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes E$ as a sequential colimit of finite extensions of the layers $\Sigma^j H\underline\pi_j$ ($j\ge c$) and invoke convergence. As written, the membership conclusion does not follow from the stated closure properties.

Both (A) and (B) are standard once made explicit, but each is currently a genuine logical gap in the chain that delivers the geometric half of the converse.

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Re-review: largely addressed in current `solution.tex`.
In the converse, the geometric layers are NOT extracted from $\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes E$ by taking its homotopy Mackey functors; instead each layer is **constructed** as $\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes\Sigma^j HM_j$ with $M_j=\underline{A_j}^{\mathrm{geo}}$, $A_j=\pi_j(\Phi^GE)$, via the equivalence $\Phi^{-1}$ on $\widetilde{E\mathcal P}$-local objects (L2). Geometricity of each layer is then automatic from `def:geom` (no need to prove $i_K^*$ commutes with $\pi_j$ in the sense feared). The assembly is a sequential (Whitehead) colimit of a bounded-below spectrum, and $\tau_{\ge n}$ is closed under extensions and sequential colimits, so it converges and lands in $\tau_{\ge n}$. I consider this resolved; the only loose internal step is (G2)'s $\pi_{k\ne0}$ vanishing, tracked separately in q5-26.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
## Both (A) and (B) made fully explicit in the geometric-part assembly.

I reworked the "geometric part" of the converse so that neither gap is left implicit. Concretely:

**(A) Layer geometricity — no need for $i_K^*\underline\pi_j=\underline\pi_j i_K^*$.**
As the prior re-review noted, the layers are *constructed*, not extracted: for $A_j:=\pi_j(\Phi^G E)$ we set $M_j:=\underline{A_j}^{\mathrm{geo}}$ (Def. `def:geom`), so geometricity is true *by construction*. By Prop. `prop:geomEM`, $HM_j$ is connective, $\widetilde{E\mathcal P}$-local, $\Phi^G HM_j\simeq HA_j$, and $\Phi^{-1}(\Sigma^j HA_j)\simeq\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes\Sigma^j HM_j$ by (L2). At no point is the spectrum-level vanishing $i_K^*(\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes E)\simeq0$ pushed through $\underline\pi_j$; the feared commutation is simply not on the path. So (A) is moot.

**(B) Limit vs. colimit — now an explicit bounded-below convergence to a sequential colimit.**
I replaced the bare assertion "$\Phi^G E$ admits a Whitehead filtration" with three labelled steps:
- *Step G-1*: $\Phi^G E$ ($c$-connective) is presented as the **skeletal/cellular** tower $F_q=\mathrm{sk}_q\Phi^G E$ with transition maps genuine subcomplex inclusions (a *colimit* diagram), cofiber layers $\Sigma^{q+1}H\pi_{q+1}$, and a comparison map $\theta:\operatorname{colim}_q F_q\to\Phi^G E$ proved to be an equivalence via $\pi_m$ commuting with sequential colimits + stabilization for $q\ge m$ (this is the bounded-below convergence). The text explicitly flags this is a colimit, NOT a Postnikov limit.
- *Step G-2*: transport along $\Phi^{-1}$, which (being an equivalence of $\infty$-cats by (L2)) preserves all colimits and cofiber sequences, giving $Y=\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes E\simeq\operatorname{colim}_q\Phi^{-1}F_q$ as a genuine sequential colimit in $\mathrm{Sp}^G$.
- *Step G-3*: membership by induction on $q$ (closure under cofibers/extensions) + closure of $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$ under sequential colimits, which is exactly closure operation (o1). The paragraph explicitly states only colimit/extension closure is used and no limit closure is claimed.

This directly answers the critic's "a localizing subcategory is closed under colimits and extensions, not under arbitrary limits": the Postnikov *limit* is never used; the object is built as an honest colimit.

I also corrected a latent slip in my own draft (I had written $F_q:=\tau_{\le q}$, which is the limit tower) to the skeletal tower $\mathrm{sk}_q$, the correct colimit system.

Brace/display-math balance checks pass; no LaTeX toolchain available to compile here. I consider the issue resolved.
